summary: [macOS] Touchpad 3D panning is not enough sensitive

description.text:
### Tested versions

- Reproducible in Godot 3
- Reproducible in Godot 4

### System information

Godot v4.5.dev (de3521ea7) - macOS Unknown (16.0.0) - Multi-window, 1 monitor - OpenGL 3 (Compatibility) - Apple M3 Max - Apple M3 Max (16 threads)

### Issue description

The sensitivity of the panning is painfully slow. The idiom is to usually be able to make the travel relative to the "real" distance travelled by the fingers. But here, it's only moving at a fraction of the distance.

At least, **the travel speed of the camera while rotating should match the travel speed of the camera while panning** (most of the time).

summary: FileDialog file_selected signal very slow, when use_native_dialog=true

description.text:
### Tested versions

Found in:
v4.4.stable.official [4c311cbee]

### System information

Windows 11

### Issue description

After selecting files and confirming with "OK" it takes ~5-7 seconds until the "file_selected" signal fires, leading to significant lag.


### Steps to reproduce

Specifically it seems to occur if the canceled, confirmed signals are connected or exclusive is set to true. So any of these lines will trigger it:
```.py
dialog.exclusive = true
dialog.canceled.connect(freeme)
dialog.confirmed.connect(freeme)
```

Here is my entire dialog setup for reference:
```.py
var dialog = FileDialog.new()

dialog.use_native_dialog = true
dialog.access = FileDialog.ACCESS_FILESYSTEM
dialog.file_mode = FileDialog.FILE_MODE_OPEN_FILE
var freeme = func(): dialog.queue_free()
dialog.exclusive = true
dialog.canceled.connect(freeme)
dialog.confirmed.connect(freeme)
dialog.close_requested.connect(freeme)

dialog.filters = ["*.json; JSON text file;text/json",
	"*.tres; Godot resource file;text/tres"]
	
dialog.file_selected.connect(func(path):
	pass # irrelevant stuff
)

add_child(dialog)
dialog.show()
```

### Minimal reproduction project (MRP)

Please see code above

summary: Inconsistent behavior when renaming files

description.text:
### Tested versions
v4.6.dev2.official [7864ac801]
v4.5.dev5.official [64b09905c]

### System information

w11

### Issue description

I have the option "Activate a window by hovering over it with the mouse" enabled.

![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/2269e761-8a4a-4db3-9e70-0b724354f857)

When I rename a file, Godot initially displays the file name correctly, but when the window focus changes, Godot seems to show that the file can no longer be renamed. However, this is not actually the case — you can still continue renaming the file even though Godot no longer shows the file name as being in a "renamable" state.

Please watch this video: you'll see that the file is renamed to "hello" even though Godot no longer shows the character input field or the renaming process.

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/3ca0dde7-25ed-402d-8885-3f93b20ed95b

This does not happen in 3.x.

The issue: As I understand it, the problem is that Godot thinks it lost focus during the file renaming process, but in reality, the file can still be renamed.

### Steps to reproduce

Enable the Windows option mentioned at the beginning of the report.
Select a file and click "Rename".
Move the cursor outside the file's text field.
Godot will appear to show that renaming is no longer possible.
Type the new file name and press Enter.
The file will have been renamed.

summary: Using "Make Unique" on a script creates a new script that cannot be saved as built-in.

description.text:
### Tested versions

Reproducible in 4.4.1.stable, 4.5.dev3

### System information

Godot v4.5.dev3 - Windows 11 (build 26100) - Multi-window, 1 monitor - Vulkan (Forward+) - dedicated NVIDIA GeForce GTX 1660 SUPER (NVIDIA; 32.0.15.7652) - Intel(R) Core(TM) i7-10700F CPU @ 2.90GHz (16 threads)

### Issue description

When using "Make Unique" on an attached script, it replaces the current script with a new script (which is expected), but the new script can _only_ be saved as a file, not as a built-in. This is frustrating when the user is intending to for example duplicate a built-in script to create another built-in script.

This behaviour is also inconsistent with how other embedded resources behaviour when "Make Unique" is used on them. For example, when you use "Make Unique" on a Material, it creates a new embedded Material in its place, and saving it into a new file is an additional, optional action the user can perform.

The expected behaviour for scripts would be to create a new built-in script in its place, and saving that script to a new file would be an additional, optional action.

![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/a29a8a63-0524-4352-9fe2-6e9150d2b57b)

### Steps to reproduce

- Open the `test.tscn` scene in the attached project.
- Inspect the `test` node.
- Right click on its script field, and click "Make Unique".
- Notice that it creates a new, unsaved script with no name or resource path.
- Hit `Ctrl-S` to save the script, and observe that it opens up a file dialog. It is not possible to save this new script as a built-in, despite the fact that it was created by making a built-in script unique.

### Minimal reproduction project (MRP)

[bug-report.zip](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/20665875/bug-report.zip)

summary: TileMapLayer does not emit changed signal when cells are changed

description.text:
### Tested versions

### Issue description

The documentation for `TileMapLayer` says this about the `changed` signal:

> Emitted when this TileMapLayer's properties changes. This includes modified cells, properties, or changes made to its assigned TileSet.

However, the signal is not emitted when changing cells. Changes to other properties cause the signal to be emitted normally.

### Steps to reproduce

1. Download and edit the minimal reproduction project
2. Edit the `main.tscn` scene
3. Edit the existing `TileMapLayer` by adding and removing cells in the 2D editor
4. Look for the message `TileMapLayer changed` in the output panel. It should be printed every time you modify any cells

summary: Sampling an HDR gradient ignores the source_color flag

description.text:
### Tested versions

- Reproducible in: v4.4.1.stable

### System information

Godot v4.4.1.stable unknown - CachyOS Linux #1 SMP PREEMPT_DYNAMIC Sat, 07 Jun 2025 05:32:41 +0000 on Wayland - X11 display driver, Multi-window, 1 monitor - Vulkan (Forward+) - integrated AMD Radeon 680M (RADV REMBRANDT) - AMD Ryzen 7 7735HS with Radeon Graphics (16 threads)

### Issue description

Sampling an HDR gradient using e.g. a GradientTexture1D using a `source_color` flag behaves as if that flag is not present. This means that two otherwise identical gradients only differing in "Use HDR" setting produce noticeably different results when sampled.

Here's a screenshot from the repro project demonstrating the problem:

![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/69a2cbcc-a14b-4c2b-9e53-8c6cda588cbb)

Gradient used - all colors are LDR:

![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/1f2a9359-301a-4eef-9f9b-867eecd9f9a5)

Left sphere uses a `source_color` flag; the middle one omits it; the right one uses it but has the HDR gradient option turned on, unexpectedly being like the middle sphere instead of the left one. All spheres are unshaded; shaders used just directly assign sampled color to albedo.

### Steps to reproduce

* Write a shader that samples a texture with a `source_color` hint.
* Supply a `GradientTexture1D` with an LDR gradient as a source
* Toggle the "Use HDR" flag on the gradient and observe that rendering results change

### Minimal reproduction project (MRP)

[broken-source_color-showcase.zip](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/20662548/broken-source_color-showcase.zip)

summary: Fix visible ratio when displaying all characters in Label

description.text:
When changing the visible characters property of a Label, the visible ratio property updates accordingly. However, when  visible characters is reset to -1 (meaning display all characters) **after** entering some text , the ratio is incorrectly set to 0 (meaning show nothing).

Do note that the editor still displays the text even though visible_ratio is 0. Running the scene gives an empty label, which is rather confusing.

This PR should fix this by setting the ratio to 1 when setting the visible characters to -1.

summary: GPUParticles2D: Draw Order does not behave as expected when emitting particles by script

description.text:
### Tested versions

Found in 4.4.1.stable.
Reproducible in 4.5.dev5.

Found using the forward_plus rendering method.

### System information

Godot v4.4.1.stable - Windows 11 (build 26100) - Multi-window, 2 monitors - Vulkan (Forward+) - dedicated NVIDIA GeForce RTX 3080 (NVIDIA; 32.0.15.6094) - AMD Ryzen 7 5800X 8-Core Processor (16 threads)

### Issue description

When emitting particles using **GPUParticles2D.emit_particle()** in a gdscript, the resulting set of particles does not reliably respect the chosen Draw Order mode. This happens for all Draw Orders (Index, Lifetime, and Reverse Lifetime).

When using Index, the effective draw order is seemingly random, particularly as more particles have been drawn.

When using Lifetime or Reverse Lifetime, the effective draw order is still seemingly random, but it also changes over time, causing particles to jump in front of or behind one another as time progresses.

### Example

This video demonstrates the behavior when Draw Order is set to Index. Each particle should be rendered on top of all previous particles, but instead, each particle is sometimes behind all other particles, sometimes in front of all other particles, and sometimes somewhere in the middle of the stack.

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/c3dda38a-a012-4036-bab9-d87fae289fbf

### Steps to reproduce

Create a GPUParticles2D node and set a texture. Set the draw order to Index (or anything else), and set the lifetime to something long enough for many particles to overlap. Set amount high enough to show many particles. Add a script that emits particles, whether all at once in the same frame or gradually over multiple frames. Observe the way the particles overlap.

Example code:
```
extends GPUParticles2D

var timer = 0
var step = 0
var coords: = []
var pause = 0.1 # seconds

func _ready() -> void:
	# Generate a spiraling set of coordinates
	var rIncrement = 1
	var thetaIncrement = 10 # degrees
	var centerCoord = Vector2(500,300)
	var theta = 0
	var r = 0
	for i in 300:
		coords.append(Vector2(
			centerCoord.x + cos(theta) * r,
			centerCoord.y + sin(theta) * r
		))
		theta += thetaIncrement * PI/180
		r += rIncrement
	# Set amount high enough to render one particle at each coordinate
	amount = coords.size()

func _process(delta: float) -> void:
	# Draw a new particle every so often, in the spiraling order the coordinates were generated in _ready()
	timer+=delta
	if timer>=pause:
		timer=0
		if step<coords.size():
			emit(coords[step])
			step+=1

func emit(pos: Vector2):
	emit_particle(Transform2D(0,pos),Vector2.ZERO,Color.WHITE,Color.WHITE,EmitFlags.EMIT_FLAG_POSITION)
```

### Minimal reproduction project (MRP)

This MRP was used to generate the video above. Just Run Current Scene on demo_bug.tscn to see it in action.

### Issue description

upon deleting the words covered by the autocompletion suggestions, accepting a suggestion will write the number of characters typed as the starting characters of the suggestion, to the right of the cursor, and the remaining characters of the suggestion on the left of the cursor, regardless of which characters were used in the fuzzy find.

summary: Window size changes when in Movie Mode aren't handled correctly

description.text:
### Tested versions

- Reproducible in: 4.5dev5

### System information

Debian GNU/Linux 12.10

### Issue description

Running a project in embed mode at a different resolution than defined in the project, produces AVI files with incorrect resolution and aspect ratio.

The problem is that in embed mode, the initialization of MovieWriter sometimes receives the project viewport size, and sometimes the embedded viewport size. But the  frames sent have always the embedded viewport size.

There was a similar error, same effect, different cause, that was reported [in the forum](https://forum.godotengine.org/t/movie-maker-mode-produces-strange-recordings-when-trying-to-use-a-different-resolution/62445) and #71254.

**Edit:** The issue doesn't happen only in embed game mode, it can happen at anytime if we're not careful to avoid window size changes. We should probably think of a way to handle window size changes in movie mode, implement a way to prevent them, or warn the user when it happens. If the movie writer isn't prepared to handle resolution changes, it could even make Godot crash. See comments below.

### Steps to reproduce

- Open any project.
- Make the viewport size in the Project Settings big enoough so that it has to be scaled down to fit the embedded window.
- Enable Movie Maker mode.
- Run the project.
- Check the resulting video.

This might take several tries because it doesn't always happen. In my case it happens like 4 out of 5 times. It looks like a race condition between the embedded process and the MovieWriter initialization.

### Minimal reproduction project (MRP)

Any project. The viewport has to be big enough to not fit the embedded window.

summary: broken WEB build with a particular `default_bus_layout.tres` and `export/convert_text_resources_to_binary=true`

description.text:
### Tested versions

- reproducible in: v4.4.1.stable.official [49a5bc7b6]

### System information

Godot v4.4.1.stable - macOS Sequoia (15.5.0) - Multi-window, 1 monitor - Metal (Mobile) - integrated Apple M3 Pro (Apple9) - Apple M3 Pro (11 threads)

### Issue description

I created a project with a particular set of audio buses and effects which turned out to break web export. This bus configuration seems to break any project (even a clean one), as long as the project has setting `export/convert_text_resources_to_binary=true` (which is the default).

Note: it took me a while to understand what is breaking my project, given that this does not happen in the remote deploy session (because it unsets the text-to-binary conversion). Possibly, this issue may be related to [this discussion](https://forum.godotengine.org/t/project-works-when-launched-from-ide-fails-when-built/42725) from which I found out about the text-to-binary setting in first place.

### Steps to reproduce

- create a new project and set up a default scene (can be empty)
- put the following bus layout in `default_bus_layout.tres`:
```
[gd_resource type="AudioBusLayout" load_steps=2 format=3 uid="uid://dfrhco6nluji5"]

[sub_resource type="AudioEffectLowPassFilter" id="AudioEffectLowPassFilter_j3pel"]
resource_name = "LowPassFilter"
cutoff_hz = 6121.0

[resource]
bus/0/volume_db = -0.030508
bus/1/name = &"bus 1"
bus/1/solo = false
bus/1/mute = false
bus/1/bypass_fx = false
bus/1/volume_db = -0.030508
bus/1/send = &""
bus/2/name = &"bus 2"
bus/2/solo = false
bus/2/mute = false
bus/2/bypass_fx = false
bus/2/volume_db = 0.0
bus/2/send = &""
bus/2/effect/0/effect = SubResource("AudioEffectLowPassFilter_j3pel")
bus/2/effect/0/enabled = true
```
- export the project as WEB
- host the exported app, e.g. with `python3 -m http.server 8000` (**important:** do not use the remote deploy host in Godot as it switches text-to-binary flag to `false`)
- connect to your server with browser (I'm using Firefox)

summary: Ruler Mode Enhancement for 3D Scene

description.text:
Inspired by the ruler tool in 2D scene, this PR implements a set of helper lines around the start and end point of the ruler tool with helper measurement labels to help users to identify ruler position in the 3d scene. This additional feature is visible when holding the `SHIFT` key.

This is the second solution discussed under the issue conversation. Key differences between this solution and the first solution is it always draws the X-Z helper lines at the ruler point with minimum `Y` value. This solution also hides measurements and angle labels if their value less than `0.0001`, as well as hiding the XZ line and unnecessary angle labels when using the ruler on a 2D plane, while the other solution uses a unified angle margin to control measurement and angle label hiding.

summary: Export resource setter not called at runtime when using "New Resource" from the editor

description.text:
### Tested versions

- Reproducible in all 4.x

### System information

Godot v4.4.1.stable - Windows 10 (build 19045) - Multi-window, 2 monitors - Vulkan (Forward+) - dedicated NVIDIA GeForce RTX 3070 (NVIDIA; 32.0.15.6590) - AMD Ryzen 7 1700X Eight-Core Processor (16 threads)

### Issue description

There is an inconsistent behaviour when setting export tagged resources from the editor while the game is running.

From this declaration:
```gdscript
extends Node2D

@export var SomeResource : Gradient :
	set(value):
		print("Setting SomeResource from %s to %s" % [SomeResource, value])
		SomeResource = value
		print("Value has been set\n")
```

- Load / Quick Load --> setter function called
- Clear (reset to default value) --> setter function called
- New Resource --> **setter function not called**

A cumbersome workaround is to use New Resource, save it, then Quick Load it to trigger the setter function.

### Steps to reproduce

From the MRP:
- Launch the game
- Verify that the setter works by loading the included "new_gradient.tres"
- Verify that the setter works by clearing the resource.
- Verify that the setter is not called by setting the resource to a New Gradient

The output will be:
```
Set SomeResource
Setting SomeResource from <null> to <Gradient#-9223372004474747564>
Value has been set

Set SomeResource
Setting SomeResource from <Gradient#-9223372004474747564> to <null>
Value has been set

Set SomeResource <-- Setter not triggered
```

### Issue description

AcceptDialog's buttons position is not always centered while the OK button is hidden. Here's the code attached to an AcceptDialog and the correspoding results:

```gdscript

var confirmButton : Button

```

Other combinations creates different results. Removing the 'right' flag fixes the issues, but obviously swaps the buttons.

```

	cancelButton = add_button("Cancel")
```

But again, removing the 'right' flag modify the behavior and uncenter them.

### **Example 4** 
```gdscript
func _ready() -> void:

	confirmButton = add_button("Confirm")
	cancelButton = add_button("Cancel")
```
![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/81069aef-048d-460e-a89d-7f535bd4ea7f)


Here's a final comparison with the OK button not hidden for example 1 and 4. My guess is that the OK button doesn't hide correctly and still occupy a space in the button row with zero width.

The second and third examples kind of works because the hidden OK button is in the middle and doesn't break the simmetry, but it creates a larger gap. Of couse it's not an engine breaking issue the behavior across buttons in the AcceptDialog is not consistent.

### Issue description

Intermediately, when running my project, it appears that keyboard input isn't processed right away. As an example, I have a CharacterBody2D hooked up to use Input.get_vector() with WASD. summary: Add "Use Local Space" option to the 2D editor

description.text:
Closes https://github.com/godotengine/godot-proposals/issues/6799.

This adds in a "Use Local Space" button to the 2D editor like the 3D editor currently has. By default, Godot uses local space in the 2D editor anyway, so the actual functionality of this PR comes from turning it off, which allows you to do global space transformations. For global translations, this works exactly as you'd expect. For global scale, I made it so that when you scale along the x-axis, it attempts to preserve the height of the object, and when you scale along the y-axis, it attempts to preserve the width of the object. I personally find it intuitive, but there might be a more obvious way that I'm missing, so I'd really appreciate feedback on this.

Keep in mind that by default, "Use Local Space" is on, so there shouldn't be any immediate difference.

### Issue description

When creating a Resource that contains another Resource as one of its exported properties, the defined script of the contained Resource will be removed upon reloading project. ```


in your FileSystem in editor create a new `TestTres` and save its .tres file somewhere.


Open that .tres file, set its "something" property to a new `ResTest`, and maybe even edit the exported property found within that, then save.

summary: Resources that inherit from a global class are not saved properly

description.text:
### Tested versions

- Reproducible in custom 4.5 build from commit bd94fc7
- Not reproducible in custom 4.5 build from commit 51b1775

### System information

Godot v4.5.custom1.mono (26df04377) - Windows 11 (build 26100) - Multi-window, 2 monitors - Vulkan (Forward+) - dedicated NVIDIA GeForce RTX 3080 (NVIDIA; 32.0.15.7283) - AMD Ryzen 7 5800X 8-Core Processor (16 threads)

### Issue description

Resources that inherit from a global script class are not saved properly anymore. This affects both GDScript and C#. Non-global scripts are NOT affected.

Affected resources show none of the custom properties in the inspector and can no longer be accessed from other scripts:
![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/530018b2-1261-4eda-9a64-b35e4149082a)


---

From what I can tell. the issue is that the `script` property is now skipped when saving global resources, because the code thinks it matches the default value.

In the code that checks whether a property has the same value as its default value, the new function (`PropertyUtils::get_property_default_value`) that is used to fetch that default value no longer returns null for the `script` property if that script is a global class. This causes the equals check to pass and so the `script` property is skipped for saving.

https://github.com/godotengine/godot/blob/42c7f144225fa3992ceda284ad8f8edcbd1e7455/scene/resources/resource_format_text.cpp#L1934-L1939

### Steps to reproduce

1. Create a global class that inherits from resource with either GDScript or C# (or use the MRP which has scripts for each)
2. Create an asset with this new class you created
3. Save the asset
4. Restart the godot editor
5. Observe that when you inspect the resource, none of its properties are displayed anymore

summary: When `SkeletonIK3D` does not have a target set it will continue to refer to the old global pose in world space

description.text:
### Tested versions

Godot v4.3, v4.4 (using GL Compatibility rendering backend)

### System information

Godot v4.4.1.stable - Windows 11 (build 26100) - Multi-window, 2 monitors - OpenGL 3 (Compatibility) - NVIDIA GeForce RTX 4060 Ti (NVIDIA; 32.0.15.6094) - 12th Gen Intel(R) Core(TM) i5-12600K (16 threads)

### Issue description

The pose of a `Skeleton3D` node becomes distorted when its parent `Node3D` is translated at runtime.

My scene is structured with a `Skeleton3D` (and its associated mesh) parented to a `Node3D` which acts as a "container" for the model. The skeleton's bones are being manipulated via script (in my case, through direct calls to `translate()` on the parent container node).

**Expected behavior:**
When the parent `Node3D` container is translated, the entire model, including the correctly posed `Skeleton3D`, should move along with it, perfectly preserving the bone poses relative to the skeleton's own coordinate space. The visual pose of the character should remain unchanged, simply shifted to a new position.

**Actual behavior:**
Upon translating the parent `Node3D`, the `Skeleton3D`'s pose breaks. Bones that are being actively posed appear to move to incorrect positions, as if their transforms are not being correctly updated relative to the parent container's new global transform. This leads to a visually corrupted and distorted mesh.

Before translate:

![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/f8ec88ac-93f5-412d-bb4b-cb18ec36af30)


After translate:

![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/3d47aff4-9639-402c-bbaa-bfb12eaf10fe)



### Steps to reproduce

1.  Set up a scene with a `Node3D` as a parent
2.  Add a `Skeleton3D` with a skinned mesh as a child
3.  Attach a script that modifies a bone's pose at runtime. 
4.  Add a UI `Button` to the scene. Connect its `pressed` signal to a function that translates the parent 
5.  Run the scene. The bone should be animating correctly.
6.  Click the button.
7.  Observe how the animation/pose of the bone becomes incorrect

summary: Android: Fix save issue when using native file dialog

description.text:
Fixes the issue where saving a file without `MANAGE_EXTERNAL_STORAGE` permission using the `native file dialog` to get the file path causes the `ERR_FILE_NOT_FOUND` error.

---
A blank file gets created by default when using the save dialog, which isn't available to the Godot app without required permission (MANAGE_EXTERNAL_STORAGE) and when trying to access that file using `FileAccess`(mediastore api) with the path returned by `FileDialog`, results in `ERR_FILE_NOT_FOUND` error (as shown in the linked issue description).
There's no way to stop this blank file creation, so this PR immediately deletes the blank file instead.

This change also brings Android's behavior in line with desktop platforms, where no blank file is created when saving using the native file dialog.

Fixes https://github.com/godotengine/godot/issues/107206

summary: File save issue using the native file dialog on Android

description.text:
### Tested versions

Issue is present since Native file diaog support is added for android in https://github.com/godotengine/godot/pull/98350

- Reproducible in 4.4.stable
- Reproducible in master

### System information

null

### Issue description

Android allows saving files in `Downloads` and `Documents` without any permission. It's works as expected if saving file directly with filepath but throws error `ERR_FILE_NOT_FOUND` if using the native file dialog to open save file UI and then try to save using the following code:
```gdscript
func _on_file_dialog_file_selected(path: String) -> void:
	print("file path: ", path)
	var file = FileAccess.open(path, FileAccess.WRITE)
	if file:
		file.store_string("This is a test.")
	else:

```

summary: Exported boolean value lost on some freshly instanced nodes from a tool

description.text:
### Tested versions

- Reproducable in: v4.4.1.stable.official [49a5bc7b6]

### System information

Windows 10 - Godot v4.4.1.stable.official [49a5bc7b6]

### Issue description

After the included tool adds an instanced scene into the test scene, the exported value of the root node's class isn't saved if toggled in the editor. Specifically if the exported boolean doesn't have a `true` default value in the script and the value in the scene is set to `true` (toggling the instanced node's value to `false` in the editor).

If the target scene is saved and one presses play (or closes and opens the scene again) the value still is `true`. After the scene has been reopened the value can be toggled to `false` like normal expected behavior.

According to the editor "Local":

![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/360c1fd2-aba1-453e-9bb4-0535af062898)

According to the editor "Remote":

![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/1dba3abf-daf9-4aa0-9d2f-96117b601504)

There's a workaround by setting all exported boolean values defaults to `true` in their scripts.

Expected behavior is that all boolean values should be respected, and particularly that the behavior would be consistent disregarding what the default value in the script or in the instanced scene value is.

### Steps to reproduce

1. Activate the inclueded tool
2. Select the `My Class - No Default - Value True` or `My Class - Default False - Value True` scene in the tool dropdown.
3. Open the `Test Scene` and select the `Parent` node
4. Click the `Add to "Parent"` button in the tool
5. Select the newly added instance under `Parent` and change its `Toggle Me` value to `false`
6. Save the `Test Scene`
7. Press play and in the `Remote` tree select the new node
8. Observe that its value is still `true`
9. Close the `Test Scene`
10. Open the `Test Scene`
11. Note that the new node value is reverted to `true` in the editor
12. Toggle the value to `false`
13. Save `Test Scene`
14. Press play and note that now it is actually also `false` in the `Remote` tree.

(Instructions also inside the MRP)

### Minimal reproduction project (MRP)

[bug--bool-not-taking - submission.zip](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/20624454/bug--bool-not-taking.-.submission.zip)

summary: TileMapLayer with Y-sort enabled renders incorrectly in SubViewport for minimap (Godot 4.4)

description.text:
### Tested versions

​​Labels​​: bug, rendering, 2D

​​Platform​​: All (tested on Windows/Linux)



### System information

​​Godot Version​​: 4.4.1-stable

### Issue description

​​Description​​
When using a SubViewport to render a 2D minimap, if a TileMapLayer has ​​Y-sort enabled​​, it is erroneously rendered even when ​​outside the SubViewport's visible area​​. This causes visual corruption in the minimap display.



### Steps to reproduce

Create a 2D scene with a TileMap node.
Enable Y-sort on the TileMapLayer.
Add a SubViewport as a minimap container.
Observe that TileMap tiles outside the SubViewport bounds are still rendered.
​​Expected Behavior​​
Only tiles within the SubViewport's visible area should be rendered.

### Minimal reproduction project (MRP)


​​Actual Behavior​​
Tiles with Y-sort enabled are rendered regardless of SubViewport boundaries, distorting the minimap.

​​Workaround​​
Temporarily disable Y-sort on layers used in minimap SubViewports (not ideal for gameplay logic).

​​Proposed Fix​​
Request to patch rendering logic in RenderingServer to respect SubViewport bounds for Y-sorted TileMapLayers.
​​Priority​​: High (breaks minimap functionality).

summary: Improve Input Map and Shortcuts editor

description.text:
- Fixes not being able to search by pressing ESC and event configuration dialog not reacting to ESC.
    - This is a regression from #76829.
- Removes double translations.
- Cleans up `InputEventConfigurationDialog` for auto-translation.
    - Part of #104574.
- Adapts to editor theme change.
    - Notably, the main reason why the lists cannot adapt to theme changes is that they use `child_node->get_theme_xxx()` in `NOTIFICATION_THEME_CHANGED` instead of calling the method directly. This redirection is not only redundant but also returns cached old values since the child nodes have not yet received the notification at that time.
- Unifies search bar boilerplate code.
- Shortcuts: disable the "erase" button if there is no event to remove.
- Input Map: always include built-in actions when searching.
    - This is the same behavior as settings searching.

summary: Cannot call GDExtension methods from within editor using a @tool script

description.text:
### Tested versions

v4.4.1.stable.official [49a5bc7b6]

### System information

Godot v4.4.1.stable - Windows 10 (build 19045) - Multi-window, 2 monitors - Vulkan (Mobile) - dedicated AMD FirePro W2100 (Advanced Micro Devices, Inc.; 27.20.21026.2006) - Intel(R) Core(TM) i5-7600 CPU @ 3.50GHz (4 threads)

### Issue description

I've created a Resource using GDExtension that I would like to access from my Godot project.  While I can call methods on this object at runtime, if it try to call them from within the editor, I get the error message
```
 ERROR: Cannot call GDExtension method bind 'alert_message' on placeholder instance.
```

### Steps to reproduce

Compile  the attached GDExtension and run the project.  This is a slight modification of the GDExtension tutorial.  

https://docs.godotengine.org/en/stable/tutorials/scripting/gdextension/gdextension_cpp_example.html

I've left out the godot-cpp directory for size, so you'll need to add it back in.  Then go into `godot-cpp` and type `scons` to compile the Godot library, then go into  the parent directory and type `scons` again to compile the extension.  The will create the PlayerStats resource object.

Next open the project in the /demo directory.  The main.tres file has an object called `check_player_stats`.  It has a property on it called `check_stats`.  If  you click it off and on in the inspector, its setter  will call `alert_message()` on the PlayerStats object assigned to the `player_stats` property.  This will produce the error message above.  However, if you run the project and press the Alert button, it will call the same method, and you will see it returns without error at run time.



### Minimal reproduction project (MRP)

[gdextension_cpp_example2.zip](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/20620937/gdextension_cpp_example2.zip)
